items.title: Unrestricted File Upload
items.summary: This critical vulnerability, part of APSB26-73, allows for unrestricted file upload, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. It has a CVSS score of 9.6.

items.title: Improper Output Encoding
items.summary: A critical improper output encoding vulnerability (CVSS 9.1) addressed in APSB26-73, which could lead to security feature bypass.

items.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
items.summary: This critical stored XSS vulnerability (CVSS 8.7) from APSB26-73 could allow attackers to inject malicious scripts and potentially hijack admin sessions.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (No Authentication Required)
items.summary: A critical incorrect authorization flaw (CVSS 8.6) in APSB26-73 that requires no authentication, allowing unauthorized users to access restricted data or perform privileged actions.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (No Authentication Required)
items.summary: Another critical incorrect authorization vulnerability (CVSS 8.2) from APSB26-73, exploitable without authentication to bypass controls.

items.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
items.summary: A critical stored XSS vulnerability (CVSS 8.1) included in APSB26-73, allowing for malicious script injection and potential privilege escalation.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization
items.summary: This critical incorrect authorization vulnerability (CVSS 7.6) from APSB26-73 could lead to security feature bypass.

items.title: Improper Input Validation
items.summary: A critical improper input validation vulnerability (CVSS 7.2) addressed in APSB26-73, which could lead to security feature bypass.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical (CVSS 9.8) unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, allowing remote code execution via a crafted cookie. Mirasvit released version 1.11.12 to fix this flaw.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization leading to Security Feature Bypass
items.summary: This high-severity (CVSS 7.5) vulnerability affects Adobe Commerce versions 2.4.9-beta1 and earlier, allowing an attacker to bypass security measures and gain unauthorized write access without user interaction.

items.title: Denial-of-Service (DoS) in Adobe Commerce
items.summary: A critical denial-of-service vulnerability in a third-party component bundled with Adobe Commerce, addressed in APSB26-49. Exploitation requires no credentials or user interaction and can crash processes.

items.title: Unauthenticated File Upload via REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that allows unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, potentially leading to remote code execution. Adobe released a fix in Magento 2.4.9, but older versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Incorrect Authorization (Full System Takeover)
items.summary: A critical vulnerability from APSB26-05 allowing incorrect authorization, which could lead to full system takeover and remote code execution.

items.title: Improper Access Control (Data Leak)
items.summary: This critical vulnerability, part of APSB26-05, involves improper access control that could result in a data leak by bypassing security filters.

items.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) for Admin Hijacking
items.summary: A critical stored XSS vulnerability from APSB26-05 that could allow malicious scripts to escalate user privileges and hijack admin sessions.
